chunks.page_summary: This page details how to connect a Rheonics sensor's Profinet output to a PLC and PC using an Ethernet network, including a recommended wiring diagram.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Connecting the Profinet output

Modified on: Thu, 24 Mar, 2022 at 10:19 PM

General
The Rheonics SME devices offer the Profinet service over the Ethernet interface.

Wiring
The SME can be connected through the Ethernet RJ45 connector as part of a network, with intermediary network devices (router, switches) or directly to an Ethernet host in a direct connection.
Connect Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC (with RCP software installed) with an Ethernet Cable. We recommend using an Ethernet Switch.

Figure 1. Communication diagram for rheonics sensor-PLC and PC.

References:
key_facts: Rheonics SME devices support Profinet service via their Ethernet interface.
The SME can be connected using an Ethernet RJ45 connector, either as part of a network (with devices like routers or switches) or directly to an Ethernet host.
An Ethernet Switch is recommended for connecting the Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC.
The PC requires RCP software and TIA Portal for this communication setup.
The document was last modified on March 24, 2022, at 10:19 PM.
diagram_labels: PC with RCP and TIA Portal
Figure 1. Communication diagram for rheonics sensor-PLC and PC.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a title and a link to a Rheonics support article on configuring an SME device with RCP using Ethernet.
key_facts: The page's primary purpose is to direct users to a support article for configuring SME with RCP.
The support article provides information on connecting to an SME device using Ethernet.
